item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh, took me awhile to see this comment. In the future, feel free to message me about tags! I appreciate your posting a comment rather than editing the tags directly.

I agree with the tags not applying, and have removed the three noted tags. Thanks for bringing that to my attention. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are some tags on this one that don't seem to apply; geological, skeletal, and animal.

I could see a case for "geological" because it does involve a terrain feature, though it seems a stretch.
But "animal" doesn't apply because we only get empty shells, there are no critters inside.
And "skeletal" is just random, no bones are even mentioned.

But I wouldn't presume to touch the tags on one of Zyn's own articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of those that you really understand after reading it a second time
Definitely +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm. It's competently written (as expected) and not bad, but a bit too predictable, and I would have liked a lot more mystery too. As it is, it just doesn't grab me. I started getting feels about halfway through, but then it felt like it just kept it there and never went anywhere exciting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much obliged, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kudos to Zyn for treading the line very carefully here between 'thing that makes you kill yourself' and 'simple reflection of external reality' - the ambiguity gives this a real sinister edge I think could have easily been missed out, and there's a vaguely dreamlike, magical realism tone to the whole piece I really liked. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers
hide

When I wrote it, I initially had the idea in mind that the ones that crumble into sand pertain to people who ended up dying natural deaths. Your thought is another possibility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it's certainly unnerving to me, given that I've struggled with suicidal impulses since I was four.

Do the ones that crumble into sand happen when something else kills a person before they decide to do it themselves?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's because I didn't grow within the context neccesary to understand the background. In my eyes, the most interesting about this article is if this is causing the suicides, or just predicting them (and while you stated that this; somehow, affects the person linked to each 1744-1 instance, it's ambigous enough to stand that question in my head). I think I was waiting a surprising twist, but I didnt feel it. And that was disappointing. I feel like this could be a lot better, but I can't think about how right now (you know, I've been busy trying to stop procrastinating my own SCP projects, and failing hard at that).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Out of curiosity, how was the end disappointing?

(I'm personally thinking of taking out the last line of the journal log, since the more I look at it, the more it seems unnecessary.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You serious?

E: corrected. I supose I upvoted before the disapointing end…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's already been too many articles like that, transparent attempts at stirring emotion without much of anything else.

Could you expand on this?

The characters in the log seem pretty generic. Especially the third, the kid. Pretty much boilerplate.

Suggestions on making them seem less so?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You, uh, upvoted, apparently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually like the subtlety of the characters in the log. You managed to get us to know what they were going through without making it seem too obvious.
+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems like it was written specifically to get people to say the word 'feels'. There's already been too many articles like that, transparent attempts at stirring emotion without much of anything else.

The characters in the log seem pretty generic. Especially the third, the kid. Pretty much boilerplate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I usually prefer when things are hinted at more mysteriously and leave room for interpretation.

I did end up trimming down the existing logs and taking one log out entirely, since I didn't want the effects to be too obvious; I kept in the journal log because I wanted there to be a solid indication of the effects. During the drafting process, I find myself becoming too vague while attempting subtlety, so I end up relying on the people I ask for critique to determine how to edit the piece for clarity.

Edit: And you're right about the fresh water. I'll change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's competently written, but feels derivative of your other "feels" articles.

Several of the preliminary versions included logs that I ended up trimming out because I felt they were excessive, and I was initially trying to go for unnerving rather than emotional. Granted, the suicide aspect lends itself to the more emotional route, and I may attempt a rewrite sometime in the future (since I do feel like I can do more with the concept, but at the moment I'm a bit tapped out on inspiration).

That said, I appreciate your help on the chat. In regards to the format though, I really wasn't quite sure how else to present this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really didn't care for this. It's competently written, but feels derivative of your other "feels" articles. I didn't really get any emotional response from reading it, and I think you can do better than parroting the same themes and formatting, Zyn. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instant +1.
Spooky as hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dont know why, but it just didn't grab me. Despite that, it's well executed. Neutral vote

P.S: Huh, first article I didnt enjoy from you Zyn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The phrase "…clear-speaking voice…" seems like a slip in tone.
But a minor one.
It does not detract from my enjoyment. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, that's what I realized at the end.

Overall I liked it; I'm not sure I would've gotten it without the last bit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one; full of +1 feelings. I thought maybe the last entry was a bit unnecessary; I usually prefer when things are hinted at more mysteriously and leave room for interpretation.

a slightly elevated salt content (compared to that of pure water)

Nitpick: pure water has no salt, so do you mean fresh water (as in a normal pond?).
Edit: at first I thought it was because it was more like sea water, but now I realized it's probably full of tears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this-a little predictable but well written/interesting. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the concept interesting, but the writing seems a bit…predictable to me. It does have a good wham line though. Ye got my upvote for concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was a bit unsure about this one. That last sentence did make me feel a little sad, but not a lot since I saw it coming. I like the concept itself though, so I'll give it a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
researchers have not determined a causative correlation

It would be "causative relationship" in this context, no?

As for the article proper, it's good for a suicide effect, but not quite my cup of tea. No vote.

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
Also, I happened to find a draft on this page, before I created it. The title of the draft was "Nuclear Disaster". Seeing as no author is listed for drafts (which, as per the rules, should never be on the mainsite) that are saved to uncreated pages, I'm going to leave the text of the article here for the original author to pick up.

Text of draft
hide
  [[>]] [[module Rate]] [[/>]] **Item #:** SCP-XXXX **Object Class:** Keter [[div style="float:right; margin:0 2em 1em 2em; width:300px; border:0;"]] |||| [[image http://inapcache.boston.com/universal/site_graphics/blogs/bigpicture/chernobyl_25th_anniversary/bp2.jpg width="300px"]] || ||||~ Reactor four after explosion, SCP-1744's main building || [[/div]] **Special Containment Procedures:** No items or personnel are to enter or exit SCP-1744 or the external containment area without approval by Site Command. Anything entering SCP-1744 must be examined both before and after contact. Anything exiting SCP-1744 is to be contained and examined; contaminated objects or resistance by any Personnel during the process will be detained by guard personnel and escorted to Site Command. Any staff enteringSCP-1744 must be equipped with a long-range GPS tracking device. Staff personnel entering SCP-1744 are to don radioactive resistant HazMat gear. As well as a SIAR, (Standard Issue Automatic Rifle.) Staff is prohibited to go into SCP-1744 Alone. Failure to obey this command brings a higher percentage of MIA or KIA status. Site command isn’t responsible for any of the lost or killed personnel that fail to comply, they will be deemed insubordinate. Groups should be no less than three and shouldn’t exceed eight persons per group. Should staff become separated, [DATA EXPUNGED], thereof they are to immediately exit SCP-1744. Any personnel that has been contaminated by the Radioactive fallout due to bite scratch or breathed the air for more than 3 hours should report to site 354 for [DATA EXPUNGED]staff or equipment lost within SCP-1744 are to be declared “missing” 48 hours after loss of GPS contact. Staff or equipment located after disappearance of GPS contacts are to be recovered whenever possible. Should the GPS beacon [DATA EXPUNGED]. --Is 30 kilometers designated for evacuation and placed under military control.-- **UPDATE:** Exclusion Zone covers an area of approximately 2,600 kilometers due to outbreak during patrol 3. **Background:** SCP-1744 is a result of what was a catastrophic nuclear accident that occurred on 26 April 1986 at the ████████ Nuclear Power Plant in Ukraine **Description:** An explosion and fire released large quantities of radioactive particles into the atmosphere, reaching for miles. The disaster is widely considered to have been the worst nuclear power plant accident in history, and is one of only two classified as a level 7 event on the International Nuclear Event Scale. The disaster began during a systems test on Saturday, 26 April 1986 at reactor number four of the ██████ plant, which is near the city of██████ and in proximity to the administrative border with ██████and the ██████River. There was a sudden and unexpected power surge, and when an emergency shutdown was attempted, an exponentially larger spike in power output occurred, which led to a reactor vessel rupture and a series of steam explosions. These events exposed the graphite moderator of the reactor to air, causing it to ignite. The resulting fire sent a plume of highly radioactive fallout into the atmosphere and over an extensive geographical area, including████. Due to the radioactive fallout in SCP-1744 hundreds of the civilians that weren’t able to leave during the evacuation became sick, the radioactive compound made many of the survivors mutate and become deformed. [[div style="float:right; margin:0 2em 1em 2em; width:300px; border:0;"]] |||| [[image http://cdn.c.photoshelter.com/img-get/I0000tQ6MMz1ctdg/s/900/ukraine-chernobyl-zone-UA100126.jpg width="300px"]] || ||||~ the city of ██████population of roughly 49,000 before evacuation, now around 35,000 infected still in the site || [[/div]] **City of ██████** Named for the nearby ██████ River, ██████ was founded on 4 February 1970, the ninth nuclear city in the Soviet Union, for the ████████ Nuclear Power Plant. It was officially proclaimed a city in 1979, and had grown to a population of roughly 49,000. Access to ██████, unlike cities of military importance, was not restricted before the disaster as nuclear power stations were seen by the Soviet Union as safer than other types of power plants. Nuclear power stations were presented as being an achievement of Soviet engineering, where nuclear power was harnessed for peaceful projects. **Nuclear fallout Disaster** The disaster began during a systems test on Saturday, 26 April 1986 at reactor number four of the ███████plant, which is near the city of ██████ and in proximity to the administrative border with██████ and the █████ River. There was a sudden and unexpected power surge, and when an emergency shutdown was attempted, an exponentially larger spike in power output occurred, which led to a reactor vessel rupture and a series of steam explosions. These events exposed the graphite moderator of the reactor to air, causing it to ignite. The resulting fire sent a plume of highly radioactive fallout into the atmosphere and over an extensive geographical area, including ██████. On 26 April 1986, at 01:23, reactor four suffered a catastrophic power increase, leading to explosions in its core. This dispersed large quantities of radioactive fuel and core materials into the atmosphere [23]:73 and ignited the combustible graphite moderator. The burning graphite moderator increased the emission of radioactive particles, carried by the smoke, as the reactor had not been encased by any kind of hard containment vessel. The accident occurred during an experiment scheduled to test a potential safety emergency core cooling feature, which took place during a normal shutdown procedure. **Evacuation Failure** On 26 April 1986 1:28 the city was under siege by the Soviet military. Evacuation starts for the next forty-eight hours, during the fiftieth hour the radioactive fallout became thick. The smoke and debris from the exploded plant littered the city in a large black plume of toxic air and poison. The mutation started, hundreds of the oncoming citizens began to mutate. The military was ordered to seal off any exits and to terminate any of the infected people. During the elimination process, most of the guards became sick due to the toxic air witch was able to penetrate the standard gas mask. A mass panic of armed guards and infected citizens erupted in to the late night. At 12:48 the city was sealed off from the public and the Exclusion Zone was set. Note: The city was undergoing evacuation for 3 day when the Soviet Union called off the evacuation due to radioactive fallout becoming fatal within 52 hours of the operation. **Death Toll** There is no set number of infected or dead citizens due. 250 MIA soldiers (estimate) 450 Infected soldiers deemed KIA. (Estimate) **Mutation and birth defects** ,,diseases associated with Radioactive Fallout,, * All cancers * Non-malignant thyroid nodular disease * Parathyroid adenoma * Posterior subscapular cataracts * Tumors of the brain and central nervous system Side-affects • Loss of hair • Skin becomes yellow-white and or green yellow-white • Skin bruises easily • Tumors full of toxic waste form all over body o Common areas are face, neck, and chest • Skin tends to burn when in direct sunlight **Addendum SCP-XXXX-01** [[collapsible show="+ Show Test Log A-1" hide="- Hide Test"]] [__**Test A-1 - 21 June 1986**__ > **Subject:** Reentering SCP-XXXX > **Procedure:** Group of class-D personnel (3) Equipped with HazMat Suits and gas masks (Class 3), military issued backpack with flashlight, matches, two days’ worth of MRE's. An SIAR with 3 magazines of ammunition and an state-of-the-art helmet cam (first of its kind). Entered at 00:01 > **Results:** All class-D personnel were deemed MIA after entering the apartment building 23. Class-D personnel were unable to fire off their weapons, were dragged into the building and were not seen again. Video record was damaged during dragging of class-D personnel down a flight of stairs. Video record lasted 34 minutes before video was cut due to lack of visual or audio. > **Analysis:** Failed Attempt. SCP-1744 occupants were not hostile till the first team of Class-D personnel got close to them. Once the occupants noticed that they were not infected they were brutally beat to death as well of [DATA EXPUNGED]. SCP-1744 occupants can only be seen in the windows of the building or in dark areas of the city of ██████ during the day. Which SCP-1744 is located nearby which is include into SCP-1744 because of the Radioactive Fallout.] [[/collapsible]] [[collapsible show="+ Show Test Log A-2" hide="- Hide Test"]] [__**Test A-2 - 23 June 1986**__ > **Subject:** Search And Recover > **Procedure:** Eight men team of four level 4 staff and four Class-D (trained in combat) Equipped with HazMat Suits and gas masks (Class 3), military issued backpack with flashlight, matches, two days’ worth of MRE's. An SIAR with 3 magazines of ammunition and a state-of-the-art helmet cam (first of its kind). Entered at 03:00 > **Operation:** > *03:05*: Group 2 entered SCP-1744 in a armored black van. > *03:10*: Pulled up to building 23. (no sight of occupants or Former Class-D Personnel) > *03:14*: Entered the building. > *03:15*: Load screaming noises are heard. > *03:16*: Start down the stair well. (Former group was seen dragged down) > *03:19*: Becomes too dark to see, Switches to flash lights. > *03:21*: Reach the bottom of stair well, blood is spotted beginning down the last five stairs (Screams seize and becomes quiet) > *03:26*: Starts to follow blood trail. > *03:29*: Reach a doubled door doorway, radios' command for permission to continue on mission. > *03:30*: Permission is granted. > *03:30.4*: One of Class-D personnel trips over former Class-D's Helmet cam. > *details*: Camera power LED turns from red to green (as if it was turned on) Group checks with Command if power was restore. > *03:34*: Command denies any restoring of power to camera. > *03:38*: Faint cries from behind the double doors become audible. > *03:40*: Examination of door begins; doors are lock and covered in blood. > *03:42*: Doors are breached and continue on mission. > *03:44*: Former class-D personnel bodies are found.(mangled) > *03:45*: Deafening screams fill the room and SCP-1744 occupants emerge surrounding them. > *03:45.7*: Group 2 starts to begin termination of hostile occupants as they retreat to the stair well. > *03:47*: Group 2 retreats up and out of the apartment building. > *03:50* Meanwhile contacting command A large cry echoes throughout the town alerting SCP-1744's occupants in neighboring builds. > *03:50.5* Request to fast extract is sent to command. > *03:51* Request is denied. > [DATA EXPUNGED] > *08:00* Group is recovered. > **Results:** Operation is successful. > **Analysis:** Two class-D personnel and one level four guard was killed during the operation. >**Note** For radio chatter please refer to "Test log Radio chatter Group 2". [[/collapsible]] [[collapsible show="+ Show Test log Radio chatter Group 2 " hide="- Hide Test"]] [__**Test A-2 Radio Chatter Log - 23 June 1986**__ > **Group Leader:** "Loading into the van now, how copy?" > > **G-Command:** Copy that, mission is on standby get ready for Oscar Mike." > > **Foreword:** Radio silence > > **<Mission commenced , [03:00]>** > > **G-Command:** Commence with Operation > > **Group leader:** Copy that, Command proceeding into █████ > > **Group leader:** > > [Repeat as necessary] > > **<End Log, [optional time info]>** > > **Closing Statement:** [Small summary and passage on what transpired afterward] [[/collapsible]] ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I've just seen way too many SCPs that talk to you and this one just feels like another to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. PLUS ONE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really not feeling this one at all. It's better explained that smashing a shell leads to someone's suicide, but knowing that didn't really lend it any emotional impact.

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded: [coughs loudly]

item.title: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
Feedback on this was kinda divided in chat, but hey, here goes. Many, many thanks to Dmatix, Drewbear, Burns, and MisterFlames for looking over the initial draft, Scantron and Mann for going over the next edited version, and Woggle, Teedles, Kens, and SRegan for helping me with the more in-depth edits. Also Silber and Roget for giving it the final look over. If I forgot someone, my apologies. D: I’ve hit a bit of an inspiration slump lately, so I do appreciate all the help.

Edit: OMG AZZLEFLUX I AM SO SORRY. D: Thank you for helping me prevent this from becoming too much like my other articles.

Scantron found the picture for me. Thanks, Scanny! ETA: since I can't find the source for the image, I have replaced it with a wikimedia commons picture that's Public Domain: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Naturalis_Biodiversity_Center_-_ZMA.MOLL.342114_-_Sinum_perspectivum_(Say,_1831)_-_Naticidae_-_Mollusc_shell.jpeg

Anyway, I first thought this up when I remembered something from my childhood: apparently one way to keep a secret is to whisper it into a seashell, then throw the shell into a body of water.

I tried to focus on the fact that the Foundation inherently holds an incredible amount of influence on the world through the SCP objects it contains, and fairly often in the attempt to learn more about the skips, they end up sacrificing their own people.
